topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay stark white against the dark wood of the desk. Leo traced the lines of the genetic report with a trembling finger, his breath catching in his throat. It was a confirmation, undeniable and utterly seismic. His heart hammered against his ribs, a frantic drumbeat echoing the sudden, overwhelming reality flooding his senses. He reread the section detailing the match percentage, his eyes blurring over the precise percentages. A slow, shaky exhale escaped him, and he leaned back in his chair, the sudden weight of the knowledge pressing down on him until he felt physically unsteady. "This... this can't be right," he whispered, the sound thin and reedy in the quiet room.

[story 2]

Sarah stared at the screen, her fingers hovering over the mouse, unable to move. The data swam before her eyes—the shared markers, the undeniable biological connection. A strange, cold pressure built behind her sternum, making her grip the edge of the desk so tightly her knuckles turned white. She blinked several times, trying to process the impossible configuration of facts. A small, involuntary shudder ran through her frame. "No," she managed, the word barely audible, a fragile sound against the silence of the apartment. She pushed the chair back abruptly, the scrape of the legs against the floor sounding deafeningly loud.

[story 3]

Mark picked up the envelope, the paper feeling suddenly brittle and heavy in his hands. He unfolded the document, and the carefully constructed walls he’d built around his understanding of his own origins seemed to crumble. His gaze swept over the names and markers, and a deep, visceral wave washed over him, leaving him momentarily speechless. He dropped the paper onto the carpet; it landed with a soft thud, but the sound seemed to reverberate through his entire body. Mark stood motionless for a long moment, his jaw slack, before he finally lowered his hands, his chest rising and falling rapidly as he tried to regulate the sudden internal upheaval.
